key_insight: The paper extends structural identifiability from unknown parameters to unknown functions in differential equation models, showing when functional components can or cannot be uniquely recovered from ideal data.
engineering_relevance: This gives a theory-backed test for whether machine-learned or hybrid mechanistic models can reliably infer hidden dynamics from observations in science and engineering.
method: Generalize classical structural identifiability to functional identifiability.
Use differential algebra methods to assess identifiability in ODE-based models.
Characterize classes of models where unique functional recovery is impossible or provable.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a theoretical framework supported by differential-algebraic identifiability analysis and characterization of several common model classes, rather than a single empirical dataset.
watch_item: Functional identifiability may fail outright for broad model classes, so model structure and observation choices must be checked before trusting learned constitutive laws.
paper_title: Structural functional identifiability and model discovery in differential equation models
